questions.question: what is wrong with this JSON file? { "interface": "Fa0/1", "bandwidth": "100mb", "status": "up", "address": { "ipaddress": "192.168.1.5", "subnetmask": "255.255.255.0", "default gateway": "192.168.1.1", }

The address should have a square bracket.
There is a missing curly bracket.

answerReview.examTip: Local L2 works but remote fails? Check the host default gateway before blaming routing protocols.

questions.question: which statement best represents the JSON data? { "ipaddress": "192.168.1.2", "subnet_mask": "255.255.255.0", "defaultgw": "192.168.1.1", "routes": [ { "route": "10.0.0.0/8 via 192.168.1.10" "route": "0.0.0.0/0 via 192.168.1.1" } ] }
choices: The interface data is incorrect because it is missing a comma after the routes.
The interface data is incorrect because it is missing a set of square brackets around the second route.
The interface data is incorrect because it contains an illegal underscore character.
Nothing is wrong with the exhibit.

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "The interface data is incorrect because it is missing a set of square brackets around the second route.".

incorrect.explanation: The interface data is incorrect because it is missing a comma after the routes. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "The interface data is incorrect because …" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: The interface data is incorrect because it contains an illegal underscore character. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "The interface data is incorrect because …" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Nothing is wrong with the exhibit. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Nothing is wrong with the exhibit." without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Find the interesting octet (not 0 or 255) and apply the block size there — not always the 4th octet.

The interface data is incorrect because it is missing a subnet mask for the second IP address.

incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "The interface data is incorrect because …" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Find the interesting octet (not 0 or 255) and apply the block size there — not always the 4th octet.
